mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over consecutive 12-month periods, or forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5 percent, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending power and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with expanding industrial production indicate synchronized demand and supply growth, which validates earnings momentum and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model with a Sharpe ratio above 0.5, or positive returns occur in <45% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Equity returns are positive in >65% of high-inflation observations, or the mechanism shows no predictive power when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising wage income, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward analysis shows a negative or zero Rank IC over consecutive 12-month periods, or payroll growth fails to correlate with equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and consumer confidence, which reduces downstream economic output and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of periods where HOUST remains under 1000.0, or the threshold effect disappears in a logistic regression test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in industrial production and real GDP confirms broad-based economic strength, which reduces recession risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression, or the combined signal predicts negative returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Weak payroll growth coinciding with rising unemployment indicates labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns with >60% accuracy during contraction periods, or out-of-sample hit rate drops below 40%.
